instructions.instruction: Use this skill before writing ANY Oban worker or enqueuing jobs.
instructions.original_snippets: Use this skill before writing ANY Oban worker or enqueuing jobs.
instructions.relevant_when: When a user asks for planning work that should trigger oban-essentials.

instructions.instruction: **Define worker module** — `use Oban.Worker` with explicit `queue`, `max_attempts`, and `unique` options
instructions.original_snippets: **Define worker module** — `use Oban.Worker` with explicit `queue`, `max_attempts`, and `unique` options
instructions.relevant_when: When a user asks for planning work that should trigger oban-essentials.

instructions.instruction: **Write tests** — use `Oban.Testing` with `assert_enqueued` and `perform_job`, covering all return paths
instructions.original_snippets: **Write tests** — use `Oban.Testing` with `assert_enqueued` and `perform_job`, covering all return paths
instructions.relevant_when: When a user asks for planning work that should trigger oban-essentials.

instructions.instruction: **Never put large data in job args** — store IDs and fetch fresh data in the worker — see [Job Args Best Practices](#job-args-best-practices)
instructions.original_snippets: **Never put large data in job args** — store IDs and fetch fresh data in the worker — see [Job Args Best Practices](#job-args-best-practices)
instructions.relevant_when: When a user asks for planning work that should trigger oban-essentials.

instructions.instruction: Use `Oban.insert/1` (not `Oban.insert!/1`) and handle the error tuple — see [Enqueuing Jobs](#enqueuing-jobs)
instructions.original_snippets: Use `Oban.insert/1` (not `Oban.insert!/1`) and handle the error tuple — see [Enqueuing Jobs](#enqueuing-jobs)
instructions.relevant_when: When a user asks for planning work that should trigger oban-essentials.

instructions.instruction: Return exactly one of these from `perform/1`. Use `{:error, reason}` for retryable failures; never raise.
instructions.original_snippets: Return exactly one of these from `perform/1`. Use `{:error, reason}` for retryable failures; never raise.
instructions.relevant_when: When a user asks for planning work that should trigger oban-essentials.

instructions.instruction: **Use `perform_job/2`** — not `perform/1`. `perform_job` validates args and simulates the Oban runtime.
instructions.original_snippets: **Use `perform_job/2`** — not `perform/1`. `perform_job` validates args and simulates the Oban runtime.
instructions.relevant_when: When a user asks for planning work that should trigger oban-essentials.

instructions.instruction: **Use `assert_enqueued/1`** — verify jobs were enqueued with correct args.
instructions.original_snippets: **Use `assert_enqueued/1`** — verify jobs were enqueued with correct args.
instructions.relevant_when: When a user asks for planning work that should trigger oban-essentials.

instructions.instruction: **Use `Oban.Testing` inline mode** in test config — jobs run synchronously in the test process.
instructions.original_snippets: **Use `Oban.Testing` inline mode** in test config — jobs run synchronously in the test process.
instructions.relevant_when: When a user asks for planning work that should trigger oban-essentials.
